issues.title: Singleton service captures scoped dependency
issues.description: OrderExportJob is registered as singleton but injects scoped dependency SampleShopDbContext.
issues.recommendation: Change the consumer to a scoped lifetime or resolve scoped dependencies through IServiceScopeFactory inside a controlled scope.
issues.problemSummary: A singleton service depends on a scoped service.
issues.whyDetected:
Evidence:
- Rule: DI003
- Project: Forge.SampleShop.Application
- File: \src\Forge.SampleShop.Application\Orders\OrderExportJob.cs
- Line: 10
- Detected: OrderExportJob is registered as singleton but injects scoped dependency SampleShopDbContext.
- Applicability: Production dependency-injection composition and constructor graph.
issues.whyItMatters: A singleton captures its constructor dependencies for the lifetime of the application. Capturing scoped services such as DbContext can leak request state, break disposal, and cause concurrency bugs.
issues.recommendedPattern: Do not inject scoped services into singletons. Use scoped service lifetimes, IServiceScopeFactory, or move the work into a scoped operation.
issues.suggestedImplementation: Change the consumer lifetime to scoped, or inject IServiceScopeFactory and create a scope only when background work executes.
issues.goodExample:
builder.Services.AddDbContext<SampleShopDbContext>();
builder.Services.AddScoped<OrderExportJob>();
